research_topic: Comprehensive Agentic Workflows Best Practices: VS Code Agents, GitHub Copilot CLI, and Multi-Agent Orchestration

foundational_concepts.definition: VS Code custom agents are specialized versions of GitHub Copilot configured via `.agent.md` files that handle unique workflows, coding conventions, and specialist use cases within the VS Code editor environment.
agent_system_architecture: Agents are defined as `.agent.md` files with YAML frontmatter and Markdown instructions
Powered by GitHub Copilot (Claude Haiku 4.5 or Claude Sonnet 4.6 depending on complexity)
Can be user-invocable or sub-agents (invoked only by orchestrator)
Support multiple tool capabilities through MCP servers and custom tool definitions
Agent modes enable specialized behavior: coding mode, analysis mode, research mode, documentation mode

agent_md_file_specification.structure: YAML frontmatter with metadata + Markdown body with instructions
required_fields: tools - array of tool configurations agent can use
instructions - detailed Markdown instructions for agent behavior
optional_fields: version - semantic version for tracking updates
tags - array of keywords for discovery
agent_md_file_specification.example_structure:
name: Documentation Specialist
description: Writes, Updates, and improves documentation
tools:
  - name: file_search
    description: Search files in workspace
  - name: semantic_search
    description: Semantic search across codebase
instructions: |
  You are a documentation specialist...

built_in_tools: file_search - find files by glob pattern
create_file - create new files with content

tool_restrictions.principle: Agent access to tools should be scoped by agent role and task context
tool_restrictions.best_practice: Define explicit tool allowlists in agent configuration rather than default-allow-all
safety_considerations: File modification tools should require clear purpose/context
Terminal/shell execution requires explicit approval or safe command whitelist
External API calls need authentication validation
Always include rollback/verification mechanisms for destructive operations

tool_composition.concept: Strategic combination of multiple tools to accomplish complex tasks
tool_composition.pattern: Agent uses search tools → analyzes results → applies modifications → verifies changes
tool_composition.key_insight: Well-composed tool chains are more reliable than single complex tools; compose atomic operations
tool_composition.failure_prevention: Include verification steps after tool use; handle partial failures gracefully

agent_modes: Code generation/modification mode - specialized for coding tasks
Research/Analysis mode - optimized for investigation and synthesis
Documentation mode - focused on clear written communication
Development/DevOps mode - specialized infrastructure and deployment tasks
Multi-agent orchestration mode - routing and delegation focus

customization_approaches.instruction_engineering: Detailed Markdown instructions guide agent behavior, decision-making, and quality standards
customization_approaches.prompt_structuring: Structure instructions with clear sections: role, responsibilities, constraints, guidelines, examples
customization_approaches.behavioral_tuning: Use specific language patterns: 'must', 'should', 'avoid' to set expectations at different priority levels
customization_approaches.context_preparation: Pre-populate agent context with workspace structure, conventions, and project-specific knowledge

best_practices_for_customization: Keep instructions concise but comprehensive - balance detail with readability
Use bullets/sections for scanability - agents process structured text more reliably
Include concrete examples of desired behavior within instructions
Specify quality criteria and error-handling expectations explicitly
Document agent-specific secrets/credentials via environment variables, not instructions
Version agent instructions in git for tracking evolution and rollback capability

mcp_server_integration.concept: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers extend agent capabilities with external tools and services
integration_pattern: MCP servers are configured in VS Code settings or .copilot configuration
Agents automatically gain access to MCP tools without modification
Common MCP servers: GitHub API, web search, file systems, database queries
Custom MCP servers enable domain-specific integrations (e.g., Slack, Jira, monitoring systems)

configuration.location_vscode: Settings > Copilot > MCP Servers or in settings.json
configuration.protocol: MCP servers communicate via stdio, SSE, or other transports

single_agent_pattern.use_case: Well-defined, scope-limited tasks (e.g., documentation writing, code review)
single_agent_pattern.implementation: Create focused agent with narrow tool set and clear instructions

agent_delegation_pattern.use_case: Complex workflows requiring multiple specialists (research, documentation, DevOps)
agent_delegation_pattern.implementation: Create orchestrator agent that analyzes requests and delegates to specialists

hierarchical_agent_pattern.use_case: Multi-level coordination: orchestrator + team leads + specialists
hierarchical_agent_pattern.example: Ben orchestrator → specialized sub-agents (doc, research, git-ops, etc.)

pitfall_1_overly_broad_agent_scope.problem: Single agent tries to handle documentation, coding, research, and DevOps
pitfall_1_overly_broad_agent_scope.consequence: Reduced quality, higher hallucination, difficulty in debugging failures
pitfall_1_overly_broad_agent_scope.solution: Decompose into specialized agents with clear domain boundaries

pitfall_2_insufficient_context.problem: Agent lacks workspace structure, conventions, or project-specific knowledge
pitfall_2_insufficient_context.consequence: Generates code/docs inconsistent with project standards
pitfall_2_insufficient_context.solution: Include project context in agent instructions; reference styleguides and patterns

pitfall_3_unclear_success_criteria.problem: Agent doesn't know when task is complete or what quality threshold to meet
pitfall_3_unclear_success_criteria.solution: Define explicit success criteria, quality standards, and verification steps

pitfall_4_poor_error_handling.problem: Agent proceeds despite tool failures or incomplete data retrieval
pitfall_4_poor_error_handling.consequence: Hallucinated content, incorrect modifications, cascading failures
pitfall_4_poor_error_handling.solution: Include explicit error handling in instructions; verify outcomes before proceeding

pitfall_5_tool_misuse.problem: Agent uses wrong tool or calls tools in wrong order (e.g., modify before searching)
pitfall_5_tool_misuse.consequence: Failed operations, wasted tokens, task incomplete
pitfall_5_tool_misuse.solution: Include tool usage examples in instructions; structure workflow as sequential steps

foundational_concepts.definition: GitHub Copilot CLI brings Copilot capabilities to the terminal, enabling agentic workflows for development tasks with extensibility through skills, custom agents, hooks, and MCP servers.
cli_core_capabilities: Tool use with approval controls (interactive approval, approval flags, auto-allow)
Session management and context persistence across commands
Slash command system for steering agent behavior

copilot_cli_vs_vs_code_agents.vscode_agents: Specialized using `.agent.md` files; tightly integrated with editor UI; visual context and file trees
copilot_cli_vs_vs_code_agents.cli_agents: Terminal-based; more scripted and automation-focused; better for CI/CD and programmatic use; similar core capabilities but different interaction model

skills.description: Markdown files containing instructions that Copilot can use to perform tasks in specific contexts
structure: Supporting scripts, examples, or templates in skill directory (optional)
skills.usage_pattern: When appropriate to task context, Copilot injects SKILL.md into agent context
scope_options: Personal skills - shared across projects, in ~/.copilot/skills/
best_practices: Write clear instructions on WHEN and HOW to use the skill
Include concrete examples and reference examples
Keep skill scope focused (one primary task per skill)
Version skills in git for tracking and rollback

custom_agents.description: Specialized versions of Copilot CLI for unique workflows, coding conventions, and specialist use cases
custom_agents.invocation: CLI can automatically infer agent or user can explicitly select via /agent command
custom_agents.configuration: Similar `.agent.md` format as VS Code but with CLI-specific tool set
custom_agents.auto_infer_pattern: Configure agent with infer: true to enable automatic selection when appropriate

mcp_servers.description: Model Context Protocol servers providing tools and external service integrations
common_servers: GitHub MCP server - GitHub API access
mcp_servers.integration_pattern: MCP servers automatically available to agents; provide tools expandingagent capabilities

hooks.description: Operations performed at specific moments in CLI sessions (e.g., session start, tool execution, session end)
hooks.configuration: Defined in custom agent or CLI configuration

instructions.description: Large, task-specific instructions that guide Copilot behavior
instructions.when_to_use: When instructions are too specific/large for main prompt but smaller than full skill
instructions.placement: Can be CLI-level, agent-level, or task-level

slash_command_system.overview: Terminal commands prefixed with '/' that steer agent behavior during CLI sessions
key_commands: /agent <name> - Select or switch to specific agent
/allow <tool> - Approve specific tool for current session
/allow-all - Approve all tools for current session

tool_approval_controls.interactive_approval: Copilot prompts for approval before using tools
tool_approval_controls.approval_flags: Use CLI flags to pre-approve tools: --allow-all-tools, --allow-tool <name>

cli_best_practices.1_customize_environment: Configure skills, agents, and MCP servers for your workflow
cli_best_practices.2_plan_before_code: Use Copilot to help plan complex tasks before jumping into implementation
cli_best_practices.3_leverage_infinite_sessions: Keep CLI session open for multi-step workflows to maintain context
cli_best_practices.4_delegate_work_effectively: Break complex tasks into Copilot-delegated steps; manage context explicitly
cli_best_practices.6_structured_coordination: Define clear workflows combining role-specific agents with runtime agents

pattern_1_role_specific_agents.description: Design agents to play specific roles in your development process (architect, developer, reviewer, QA)
pattern_1_role_specific_agents.workflow_example: User request → Role-specific agent analyzes → Documentation/code output
pattern_1_role_specific_agents.benefits: Clear accountability, specialized expertise, easier to reason about
pattern_1_role_specific_agents.implementation: Create focused agent definitions for each role with explicit responsibilities and constraints

pattern_2_runtime_agents.description: Agents that execute the workflow defined by role-specific agents
pattern_2_runtime_agents.workflow_example: Feature request → Architecture agent designs → Developer agent implements → Reviewer agent validates
pattern_2_runtime_agents.benefits: Separation of planning and execution; better error recovery
pattern_2_runtime_agents.implementation: Runtime agents follow structured plans; can escalate or ask for guidance

pattern_3_hybrid_workflows.description: Combine role-specific (design layer) agents with runtime (execution layer) agents
pattern_3_hybrid_workflows.workflow_example: Complex feature → docs agent + research agent (parallel) → architecture agent → implementation agent → testing agent
pattern_3_hybrid_workflows.validation_pattern: Include verification agents that validate outputs against specifications
pattern_3_hybrid_workflows.key_insight: Document clearly what ran, which agents were involved, what ran in parallel, and validation results

integration_with_vs_code.github_copilot_agent_mode: VS Code has dedicated Agent mode leveraging same capabilities as CLI agents
integration_with_vs_code.shared_capabilities: Same tool models, MCP servers, and skill systems work across VS Code and CLI
integration_with_vs_code.workflow_pattern: Agents can be invoked from either VS Code UI or CLI depending on context
integration_with_vs_code.context_sharing: Limited direct context sharing between VS Code and CLI, but can share skills/agents via git

pitfall_1_tool_approval_confusion.problem: Users uncertain about tool approval controls; either no approvals or over-approving
pitfall_1_tool_approval_confusion.solution: Document approval model clearly; use --allow-all-tools carefully in CI/CD contexts

pitfall_2_skill_discoverability.problem: Agents don't know about available skills or when to use them
pitfall_2_skill_discoverability.consequence: Skills not leveraged; duplicated effort in prompt
pitfall_2_skill_discoverability.solution: Skills must be explicitly documented in instructions; include usage examples

pitfall_3_mcp_configuration_confusion.problem: MCP servers configured in wrong location or incorrect format
pitfall_3_mcp_configuration_confusion.consequence: Agents don't have expected tool access
pitfall_3_mcp_configuration_confusion.solution: Clear documentation on configuration paths (~/.copilot/mcp.json vs .copilot/mcp.json)

pitfall_4_session_context_loss.problem: Long CLI sessions lose context or variables
pitfall_4_session_context_loss.solution: Use /cwd to persist state; leverage session context variables explicitly

foundational_concepts.definition: Multi-agent orchestration is the coordination model that determines how multiple specialized AI agents communicate, delegate work, and collaborate to solve complex tasks
key_principles: Single agent per focused domain - agents are specialists not generalists
Clear role definition - each agent has explicit responsibilities and constraints
Explicit delegation - work routing is intentional, not automatic
Dependency management - explicit ordering of sequential vs parallel tasks
Verification at boundaries - validate outputs at agent handoff points
Transparent coordination - humans/systems can observe orchestration decisions

orchestration_vs_coordination.orchestration: Central authority (orchestrator) explicitly controls workflow and agent interactions
orchestration_vs_coordination.coordination: Agents negotiate directly with each other on work allocation and sequencing

orchestrator_worker_pattern.architecture: Central orchestrator agent fans out work to specialized worker agents
orchestrator_worker_pattern.control_flow: Orchestrator receives request → analyzes → determines which agents → delegates → coordinates results
strengths: Easy to reason about and debug
Excellent for error recovery and escalation
weaknesses: Less suitable for highly parallel, peer-to-peer work
orchestrator_worker_pattern.best_for: Most multi-agent systems; particularly good for development workflows

group_chat_collaboration_pattern.architecture: Multiple agents participate in shared conversation thread for collaborative problem-solving
group_chat_collaboration_pattern.control_flow: Initial agent proposes approach → other agents provide feedback → group converges on solution → one agent executes
strengths: Agents can challenge each other's assumptions
Good for validation and review workflows
weaknesses: Can be slow if group chat cycles many times
Requires careful prompt design to prevent circular discussions

agent_handoff_pattern.architecture: Explicit transfer of work from one agent to another based on context conditions
control_mechanisms: Conditional routing - supervisor outputs specific strings triggering conditional edges
Command objects - agents issue structured handoff commands updating state + routing
Context conditions - handoff triggered when specific variables or conditions met
strengths: Natural for escalation paths (user → tier1 support → specialist → engineering)

implementation_patterns.advanced_command: Agent issues Command object with destination + state updates
implementation_patterns.context_based: OnContextCondition checks state variables to decide next agent

plan_and_execute_pattern.architecture: Planner agent creates detailed execution plan → executor agents follow the plan
phases: Planning phase - planner analyzes request and creates structured task list with dependencies
Execution phase - execution agents work through plan, reporting progress and blockers
Verification phase - verifier validates execution against plan
strengths: Agents can operate independently following plan
weaknesses: Plans become stale if execution reveals issues
Less suitable for exploratory/research tasks with uncertain paths

hierarchical_agent_pattern.architecture: Tree-structured delegation: orchestrator → team leads → specialists
levels: Level 1 - Primary orchestrator handles request routing
Level 2 - Domain team leads manage specialists within domain
hierarchical_agent_pattern.use_case_example: Enterprise deployment with docs domain, DevOps domain, research domain

swarm_pattern.architecture: Decentralized emergent coordination; agents operate semi-autonomously with minimal central control
characteristics: Agents have local goals and local communication
swarm_pattern.use_cases: Rarely suitable for LLM agents in typical business workflows due to unpredictability
swarm_pattern.caution: Swarm patterns work better for deterministic systems; LLM agents need explicit coordination

orchestrator_analysis_process: 1. Understand request - parse user intent and extract key context
2. Identify work type - classify request as documentation, coding, research, DevOps, etc.
3. Decompose workflow - break complex requests into discrete tasks with explicit dependencies
4. Identify parallel opportunities - find independent sub-tasks that can execute concurrently
5. Route to specialists - delegate each task to appropriate specialist agent with full context
6. Coordinate execution - orchestrate sequential and parallel execution
7. Synthesize results - collect outputs and summarize for user
delegation_best_practices: Provide full context - don't assume specialist knows project conventions or user intent
Set clear success criteria - specify what good outcome looks like
Include relevant workspace state - path to related files, project structure, style guides
Enable error recovery - specify fallback options if task encounters blockers
Track dependencies - explicitly specify which tasks must complete before others start

context_passing.approach_1_explicit_context: Orchestrator passes full context to each agent (can be verbose but clear)
context_passing.approach_2_shared_memory: Agents read shared workspace memory (`.tmp/`, workspace root) for context
context_passing.approach_3_file_based: Results written to files agents can discover and read as needed

agent_specialization_and_role_definition.specialization_principle: Agents should have narrow, well-defined expertise areas rather than broad general capability
benefits: Easier to test and validate specialist outputs
Simpler to debug and improve agents
Better token efficiency (shorter, more focused instructions)

role_definition_framework.element_1_domain: What specific area does this agent specialize in? (e.g., documentation, research, DevOps)
role_definition_framework.element_2_responsibilities: What is this agent accountable for delivering?
role_definition_framework.element_3_constraints: What is this agent NOT allowed to do?
role_definition_framework.element_4_tools: What specific tools does this agent have access to?
role_definition_framework.element_5_success_criteria: How do we know this agent succeeded?
role_definition_framework.element_6_escalation: When/how does this agent escalate to orchestrator?

@ben.responsibilities: Analyze requests, route to appropriate agents, coordinate workflows
@ben.constraints: Never performs implementation work directly; always delegates to specialists
@ben.tools: Orchestration: invoke agents, read shared memory, coordinate execution

@doc.responsibilities: Write, update, improve READMEs, guides, API docs, comments
@doc.constraints: Only modifies documentation; doesn't write application code
@doc.tools: File search, semantic search, create/edit files, code analysis

@research.domain: Investigation and synthesis of complex topics
@research.responsibilities: Research agentic patterns, VS Code APIs, best practices; create structured findings
@research.constraints: Research only - doesn't implement solutions; always cites sources
@research.tools: Web search, content extraction, codebase grep, synthesis

direct_communication.when_suitable: Sequential work where downstream agent depends on upstream output
direct_communication.limitation: Violates clean separation; couples agents; makes orchestration opaque

orchestrator_mediated.mechanism: All inter-agent communication flows through orchestrator
orchestrator_mediated.when_suitable: Most workflows; preserves clear accountability and visibility
orchestrator_mediated.implementation: Agent reports to orchestrator → orchestrator delegates to next agent with context

shared_memory_based.mechanism: Agents write results to shared workspace (files, database, shared temp directory)
shared_memory_based.implementation: Agent A writes output to file → Orchestrator delegates to Agent B → Agent B reads file

event_based.mechanism: Agents subscribe to events and react; event bus coordinates communication
event_based.when_suitable: Complex, reactive systems (less common in dev workflows)

inter_agent_communication_patterns.best_practice: Use orchestrator-mediated for most workflows; shared memory for parallel tasks; avoid direct communication

task_dependency_types: Sequential - Task B cannot start until Task A completes
Conditional - Task B only runs if Task A succeeded (or failed with specific error)
Parallel - Task A and Task B execute simultaneously with no dependencies
Partial ordering - Some tasks have dependencies, others are independent

explicit_dependency_declaration.pattern: Orchestrator explicitly states which tasks are dependent, independent, sequential, parallel
explicit_dependency_declaration.implementation: Include dependency graph in orchestration context
explicit_dependency_declaration.benefit: Enables efficient parallel execution; clear what can be optimized

handling_failures.approach_1_fail_fast: If Task A fails, subsequent dependent tasks do not run
handling_failures.approach_2_fault_tolerance: Some tasks can be retried or have fallback agents
handling_failures.approach_3_escalation: If critical task fails, orchestrator escalates to human

dependency_management.workspace_example: Git operations only run after all code/doc changes complete (sequential dependency)

parallel_execution_opportunities: Multiple independent sub-tasks from single complex request
Research + documentation writing (different specializations, no dependency)
Code implementation + testing framework design (can proceed in parallel with handoff)
Analysis of different system components (no dependencies between them)

implementation.approach: Orchestrator identifies independent tasks → delegates all to respective agents → waits for all to complete → synthesizes results
implementation.context_requirements: Each agent must have sufficient context to work independently
implementation.coordination: Minimal real-time coordination needed; batch up results at end

benefits: Wall-clock time reduction (significant for complex workflows)
Simpler agent implementations (no need to coordinate with each other)
pitfalls: Too much parallelization without dependency analysis → wasted work if assumptions wrong
Parallel tasks with hidden dependencies → race conditions or inconsistencies
Context explosion - too many agents operating independently lose sight of unified goal

failure_mode_1_specification_failures.description: Orchestrator misspecifies what specialists should do; agents receive unclear/contradictory requirements
failure_mode_1_specification_failures.consequence: Agents execute wrong work or produce poor quality outputs
mitigation: Explicit success criteria in every delegation
Examples of expected outputs in instructions
Clear constraints and boundaries
Verification step after agent completes work
failure_mode_1_specification_failures.research_finding: 41-86% of multi-agent systems fail in production; 79% of failures from bad specs and broken coordination

failure_mode_2_inter_agent_misalignment.description: Agents disagree on interpretation of task, expected format, quality standards
failure_mode_2_inter_agent_misalignment.consequence: Outputs incompatible; require rework; cascade failures
mitigation: Shared context defining project conventions and standards
Explicit format specifications for agent outputs
Verification agents that validate compatibility
Pre-coordination between agents before execution

failure_mode_3_tool_failures.description: Agents struggle when tools fail, return incomplete data, or behave unexpectedly
mitigation: Explicit error handling in agent instructions
Verification of tool outputs before proceeding
Fallback strategies (retry, alternative tool, escalate)
Clear error messages to help agents recover

failure_mode_4_context_loss.description: Agents work with stale or incomplete context
failure_mode_4_context_loss.consequence: Outputs inconsistent with project state; re-work required
mitigation: Fresh context retrieval at task start
Shared memory for critical project state
Refresh context for long-running agent sessions

failure_mode_5_poor_termination.description: Agents don't know when task is complete; over/under-deliver
failure_mode_5_poor_termination.consequence: Incomplete work or wasted effort on unnecessary tasks
mitigation: Explicit success criteria and completion signals
Verification step confirming task done
Clear stopping conditions in instructions

failure_mode_6_hallucinated_reasoning.description: Agents confidently make up information when uncertain
mitigation: Require agents to indicate uncertainty explicitly
Verification and fact-checking steps
Grounding in actual project artifacts, not training data

real_world_case_study_workspace_architec.system_overview: This workspace is a production multi-agent orchestration hub with clear role specialization and hierarchical coordination
orchestrator_ben_workflow: 1. Analyse - Determine work type (documentation, research, development, DevOps, etc.)
2. Decompose - Break complex requests into discrete sub-tasks with explicit dependencies
3. Delegate - Route to appropriate specialist agents with full context
4. Coordinate - Run independent tasks in parallel; sequence dependent ones in order
5. Report - Summarize what was delegated, to whom, and the outcome

specialization_implementation.@ben: Pure orchestration - never implements directly, always delegates to specialists
specialization_implementation.@doc: Documentation-only specialist - writes READMEs, guides, API docs, comments only
specialization_implementation.@research: Research-only specialist - investigates and synthesizes complex topics; cites sources
specialization_implementation.@git-ops: DevOps specialist - manages git operations with Conventional Commits validation
specialization_implementation.@ar-director: HR specialist - recruits new agents when capability gaps identified
specialization_implementation.@ar-upskiller: Training specialist - updates agent definitions with latest best practices

real_world_case_study_workspace_architec.dependency_management_example: Git operations only execute after code/doc changes complete; enforced via orchestration sequence
real_world_case_study_workspace_architec.inter_agent_communication: Orchestrator-mediated - all coordination flows through @ben; agents report completion to @ben
real_world_case_study_workspace_architec.error_recovery: Ben evaluates specialist outputs; can request rework or escalate issues
real_world_case_study_workspace_architec.scalability_approach: New specialist agents recruited on-demand when capability gaps identified (via @ar-director)
lessons_learned: Clear specialist roles enable high-quality outputs within domains
Orchestrator pattern scales well as new specialists are added
Explicit context passing (instructions, file references) crucial for agent alignment
Separation of orchestration (Ben) from execution (specialists) maintains clean architecture
File-based state sharing (.tmp/) enables inter-agent discovery and async coordination

vs_code_integration: File system and semantic search integration
orchestration_frameworks: Semantic Kernel (Microsoft) - agent framework with sequential/concurrent/group chat/handoff orchestration
LangGraph (LangChain) - structured agent graphs with conditional routing
AG2 (formerly AutoGen) - multi-agent conversations with handoff protocols
Akka - supervised agent orchestration with workflow coordination
supporting_tools: MCP servers (GitHub, web search, custom domains)
File systems and shared memory for async coordination
Observability and logging for production systems

integration_best_practices.practice_1_clear_separation: Separate orchestration (Ben) from execution (specialists); maintain clean architecture
integration_best_practices.practice_2_explicit_context: Pass comprehensive context to agents; don't assume they know project conventions
integration_best_practices.practice_3_verification_at_boundaries: Validate outputs at agent handoff points; catch errors early
integration_best_practices.practice_4_parallel_where_possible: Identify independent tasks and run them concurrently for efficiency
integration_best_practices.practice_5_documented_workflows: Clearly document orchestrator logic, agent responsibilities, and communication patterns
integration_best_practices.practice_6_scalable_specialist_recruitme: Design system to accommodate new specialist agents as capability gaps emerge
integration_best_practices.practice_7_observable_coordination: Log orchestration decisions and agent interactions for debugging and monitoring

sources.title: Creating Agent Skills for GitHub Copilot CLI
Comparing GitHub Copilot CLI Customization Features
From Prompts to Pipelines: Role-Specific and Runtime Agents
Multi-Agent Orchestration: Patterns and Best Practices for 2024
AI Agent Orchestration Patterns - Azure Architecture Center
Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail? - arXiv
Why do multi agent LLM systems fail (and how to fix) - 2026 Guide
Agent Orchestration Patterns: Multiple Architectures Explained
AI Agent Orchestration Patterns for Reliable Products
How Agent Handoffs Work in Multi-Agent Systems
A Practical Guide to LLM Failure Modes
Multi-Agent Systems: AI Agent Teams for Marketing - Digital Applied
Invoking Custom Agents in Copilot CLI
Extending GitHub Copilot Chat with MCP Servers

summary: Comprehensive research on agentic workflows spanning VS Code custom agent development, GitHub Copilot CLI extensibility, and multi-agent orchestration. Covers foundational concepts, implementation patterns, real-world case studies (including this workspace's Ben/specialist pattern), common pitfalls, and tool ecosystems. Key finding: most successful multi-agent systems use orchestrator-worker pattern with clear specialist role definition, explicit context passing, and dependency management. Failure modes concentrated in specification/coordination gaps (79% of failures).
follow_up_questions: How should inter-agent memory/context be shared in long-running coordination sessions?
What observability/monitoring is needed to debug complex multi-agent orchestration failures?
How can agent role definitions evolve as system complexity grows?
